item.title: Bhikkhus, a bhikkhu possessing two things dwells happily in this very life, without trouble, without distress, without fever; and after death, with the breakup of…
item.description:
Bhikkhus, a bhikkhu possessing two things dwells happily in this very life, without trouble, without distress, without fever; and after death, with the breakup of the body, a good destination is to be expected. What two? Restraint of the sense faculties and moderation in eating. Bhikkhus, a bhikkhu possessing these two things dwells happily in this very life... a good destination is to be expected.

Eye, ear, and nose,
Tongue, body, and mind —
A bhikkhu who guards these doors well,
Who has moderation in eating,
Experiences bodily happiness,
Experiences mental happiness.
Burning in body,
Burning in mind,
Whether by day or by night,
He dwells in happiness.

From: Itivuttaka 29
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Friends, if a bhikkhu requests: 'May the venerable ones speak to me, may I be spoken to by the venerable ones!' But if he is…
item.description:
Friends, if a bhikkhu requests: 'May the venerable ones speak to me, may I be spoken to by the venerable ones!' But if he is difficult to speak to, possessing qualities that make him difficult to speak to, hard to endure, not respectfully accepting instruction — then his fellow celibates think that bhikkhu is not worth speaking to, think that bhikkhu is not worth instructing, think that one should not place confidence in him.

From: Majjhima Nikāya 15 – Anumānasutta
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Dhamma-conduct and the holy life are called the best conduct. If one goes forth from home to homelessness, Yet if he is harsh of speech,…
item.description:
Dhamma-conduct and the holy life are called the best conduct.
If one goes forth from home to homelessness,
Yet if he is harsh of speech, fond of harming like a beast,
His life is evil, he increases the dust of defilements.

A bhikkhu who loves disputes, covered by ignorance,
Does not understand the Dhamma and Discipline taught by the Buddha.

Led by ignorance, he harms those trained in self-development,
Not knowing the defiled path that leads to hell.

Such a bhikkhu, fallen into a bad destination,
From this birth to that, from darkness to darkness,
After death comes to suffering.

From: Sutta Nipāta 2.6
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Bhikkhu, practice meditation, Do not be heedless, Do not let your mind whirl in sensual pleasures. Do not, being heedless, swallow a red-hot iron ball,…
item.description:
Bhikkhu, practice meditation,
Do not be heedless,
Do not let your mind whirl in sensual pleasures.
Do not, being heedless, swallow a red-hot iron ball,
And when burning, cry 'This is suffering!'

There is no jhāna without wisdom,
There is no wisdom without jhāna.
One who has both jhāna and wisdom
Is indeed close to Nibbāna.

Entering an empty hut,
The bhikkhu with peaceful mind
Enjoys a superhuman delight
When he sees the Dhamma clearly.

Whenever one is mindful
Of the rise and fall of the aggregates,
One experiences joy and happiness.
That is the Deathless for those who know.

From: Dhammapada 360-382
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Bhikkhus, as long as the bhikkhus share without partiality, together with their virtuous companions, all righteous, lawful gains, even down to the contents of their…
item.description:
Bhikkhus, as long as the bhikkhus share without partiality, together with their virtuous companions, all righteous, lawful gains, even down to the contents of their alms bowls, then the bhikkhus will prosper and not decline.

From: Dīgha Nikāya 16 – Mahāparinibbānasutta
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Rāhula, for one who knowingly speaks falsely, without shame and fear, I say there is no evil he will not do. Therefore, Rāhula, 'I will…
item.description:
Rāhula, for one who knowingly speaks falsely, without shame and fear, I say there is no evil he will not do. Therefore, Rāhula, 'I will not speak falsely, even for a joke' — thus, Rāhula, you should train.

From: Majjhima Nikāya 61 – Ambalaṭṭhikarāhulovādasutta
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Furthermore, bhikkhus, a bhikkhu, when walking for alms in the village or town, does not receive coarse or fine food as much as he wishes.…
item.description:
Furthermore, bhikkhus, a bhikkhu, when walking for alms in the village or town, does not receive coarse or fine food as much as he wishes. He thinks: 'I walked for alms in the village or town and did not receive coarse or fine food as much as I wished. Now my body is tired and cannot bear it. Let me lie down.' He lies down, not making an effort... unrealized. Bhikkhus, this is the fifth ground for laziness.

From: Aṅguttara Nikāya 8.80
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Having subdued the mind, well established in mindfulness, I will wander from country to country, Training many disciples. Heedful, diligent, they will practice my teaching.…
item.description:
Having subdued the mind, well established in mindfulness,
I will wander from country to country,
Training many disciples.

Heedful, diligent, they will practice my teaching.
Though you may not wish it, they will go where they go,
And there they will not grieve.

From: Sutta Nipāta 3.2
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: How is it, bhikkhu, That you have no sorrow, And yet also Have no joy? How is it that you now Sit alone in solitude,…
item.description:
How is it, bhikkhu,
That you have no sorrow,
And yet also
Have no joy?
How is it that you now
Sit alone in solitude,
Having no joy,
And also not agitated?

Truly, yakkha,
I have no sorrow,
Yet in me
No joy arises.
Though now I sit
Alone in solitude,
I have no joy,
And also am not agitated.

How is it, bhikkhu,
That you have no sorrow,
How is it that in you
No joy arises?
How is it that you now
Sit alone in solitude,
Having no joy,
And also not agitated?

Joy only comes
To one with sorrowful mind,
Sorrow only comes
To one with joyful mind.
Therefore, a bhikkhu,
Neither joyful nor sorrowful.
So, sir,
You should know this.

After a long time I see
A brahmin at peace,
A bhikkhu without sorrow,
And without joy,
Safely crossed beyond
The attachment of the world.

From: Saṃyutta Nikāya 2.18
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: - Venerable Sir, in my family there is giving. Bhikkhus who are forest-dwellers, almsfood-eaters, rag-robe wearers, Arahants or those practicing the Arahant path, Venerable Sir,…
item.description:
- Venerable Sir, in my family there is giving. Bhikkhus who are forest-dwellers, almsfood-eaters, rag-robe wearers, Arahants or those practicing the Arahant path, Venerable Sir, such bhikkhus receive alms.
- Certainly, householder, this is indeed difficult for you, a layperson enjoying sensual pleasures, bound by children, using Kāsi sandalwood, wearing and anointing with garlands, scents, perfumes, using gold and silver, to know who is an Arahant and who is practicing the Arahant path. A forest-dwelling bhikkhu, householder, if he is restless, arrogant, agitated, talkative, garrulous, unmindful, not clearly comprehending, unconcentrated, with distracted mind, loose faculties — then in that respect he is to be criticized. Nevertheless, householder, make offerings to the Saṅgha. If you make offerings to the Saṅgha, your mind will be serene. If your mind is serene, at death, with the breakup of the body, you will be reborn in a good destination, a heavenly world.

From: Aṅguttara Nikāya 6.59
📖 Read this sutta
